channel.title: DroneClear Patterns — Daily Intel Brief
channel.description: Daily UAS intelligence brief from the DroneClear PIE pipeline: gray zone entities, supply chain signals, procurement predictions, and actionable watch items for defense and public safety operators.

item.title: [PIE] April 11, 2026 — 467 airframes grounded across 25 states create $50M–$2B national exposure as gray zone vendors face regulatory extinctio
item.description:
Signal Summary: 223 active flags — 🔴 40 critical  |  🟡 95 warning

Top concern: 467 grounded airframes create $2B national replacement exposure

Lead Story

NASAO Survey: 467 Grounded Airframes Create Up to $2B National Replacement Crisis

Oregon/NASAO's February 2026 white paper revision 2 documents 467 drone airframes grounded or restricted across 23 states based on a survey of 25 state DOTs, creating a national replacement exposure ranging from $50 million to $2 billion. The grounding wave correlates with FCC Covered List enforcement, NDAA Section 1709(a)(1) implementation, and state-level bans on adversary-linked platforms. This represents the first quantified national impact assessment of the post-ban landscape, confirming that procurement paralysis is translating to operational capability loss at scale across state and local agencies.

Sources: NASAO Revision 2 White Paper

Gray Zone Entities (4)

🔴 SkyRover / Knowact

Status: On FCC Covered List; gray zone risk score 0.850 (CRITICAL); S1 and X1 models still available at retail despite ban.

Development: Teardown analysis by Jon Sawyer confirmed SkyRover app connects directly to DJI cloud infrastructure with identical encryption keys, and over-the-air update pipeline touches adversary infrastructure. Shell company filing identified as SZ Knowact Robot Technology Co Ltd. The S1 ($359) and X1 ($599) remain in retail channels despite no new FCC authorizations permitted since December 2025.

Buyer Exposure: Any agency operating SkyRover S1 or X1 faces direct data exfiltration risk to DJI-controlled cloud infrastructure and potential firmware compromise via adversary-controlled OTA updates; existing inventory violates secure data handling policies.

Action: Ground all SkyRover airframes immediately, quarantine app data from network access, initiate incident reporting under CISA guidance for potential adversary cloud exposure, and flag associated procurement contracts for legal review regarding misrepresentation of origin.

Sources: DroneClear Forge DB teardown analysis

🔴 Anzu Robotics

Status: Texas DTPA lawsuit filed February 18, 2026 in Collin County with 7 counts; no response filed as of April 4 (45+ days frozen); gray zone risk score 0.782 (CRITICAL).

Development: Teardown confirms PCB layouts align exactly with DJI Mavic 3 Enterprise, board part numbers match, and RRC01 controller is relabeled DJI hardware. Firmware is signed and encrypted with DJI cryptographic keys, meaning DJI retains root key control and built on DJI SDK. Texas is seeking injunctive relief, civil penalties up to $10K per violation, and orders requiring corrective disclosure. Product discontinued due to adversary component shortage.

Buyer Exposure: Agencies operating Anzu Raptor series face identical hardware and firmware exposure as DJI Mavic 3 Enterprise, including root key control by DJI; pending injunction could force immediate grounding; procurement contracts may be voided under deceptive trade practice findings.

Action: Suspend all Anzu Raptor operations pending Texas AG litigation outcome, treat airframes as DJI-equivalent for security classification, initiate contract termination discussions with vendor citing material misrepresentation, and preserve forensic images of firmware and flight data for potential federal inquiry.

Sources: Texas DTPA filing, Collin County, DroneClear Forge DB teardown analysis

🔴 Cogito Tech / SPECTA

Status: On FCC Covered List as of December 22, 2025; gray zone risk score 0.537 (HIGH); no new models can receive FCC authorization.

Development: Teardown confirms Specta Air has identical PCBs to DJI Air 3, same DJI P1 chipset, and RF emission reports differ only by date and logo swap. Technology licensed from DJI but licensing relationship undisclosed and not acknowledged in marketing materials. FCC has retroactive revocation authority since October 2025 vote, meaning existing authorizations could be pulled at any time.

Buyer Exposure: Agencies with Specta Air inventory operate DJI Air 3 clones with undisclosed adversary licensing; FCC retroactive revocation could eliminate legal authority to operate existing units mid-contract, stranding capital investment and creating compliance violations.

Action: Treat Specta Air as DJI-equivalent hardware, plan for mandatory grounding within 90 days as FCC exercises retroactive revocation, identify Blue UAS Framework alternatives in same performance class, and pursue vendor reimbursement under misrepresentation or fitness-for-purpose clauses.

Sources: FCC Covered List, DroneClear Forge DB teardown analysis

🔴 Autel Robotics

Status: Named in NDAA Section 1709(a)(1) alongside DJI; added to FCC Covered List December 22, 2025; gray zone risk score 0.525 (HIGH); seeking reconsideration at FCC.

Development: FCC revoked certain pre-ban authorizations in January 2026, and Autel is seeking reconsideration at FCC level. Unlike Anzu/Specta/SkyRover, Autel's technology is proprietary and not a DJI derivative, but congressional designation under Section 1709(a)(1) creates statutory ban regardless of technical origin. Autel Dragonfish fixed-wing platform is in operational use by Florida Sheriff and other law enforcement agencies as of 2021-2024 deployments.

Buyer Exposure: Agencies operating Autel Dragonfish, EVO II, or other platforms face statutory prohibition under NDAA Section 1709(a)(1) independent of FCC outcome; reconsideration petition creates temporary uncertainty but does not suspend enforcement; Florida Sheriff and similar adopters face grounding and replacement costs.

Action: Monitor Autel FCC reconsideration docket for final ruling by June 2026, maintain operational use only under explicit legal counsel clearance, develop replacement procurement specifications for Dragonfish-equivalent fixed-wing platforms in Blue UAS Framework, and budget for transition costs in FY2027 regardless of FCC outcome due to NDAA prohibition.

Sources: FY2025 NDAA Section 1709(a)(1), FCC Covered List

Supply Chain Signals (3)

🟡 FLIR Boson 640 thermal camera

Signal: 87% probability of allocation tightening in H2 2026; 15 of 52 tracked thermal camera modules (29% concentration) use FLIR Boson; 3 Boson units tracked in Forge DB; AeroVironment Switchblade production ramp identified as demand driver.

Window: 6-9 months before allocation-only distribution begins; lead times currently 12-16 weeks and extending.

Action: Pre-order 24-month Boson 640 inventory by end of Q2 2026, negotiate contract price protection with distributors before allocation triggers, and qualify FLIR Hadron 640R or Seek Thermal as second-source alternates in current development programs.

🟡 NVIDIA Jetson Orin NX companion computer

Signal: 82% probability of defense allocation-only policy in Q3 2026; 16 of 25 tracked companion computers (64% concentration) use Jetson Orin NX; 13 boards in Forge DB at risk; concentration risk model confidence 1.00.

Window: 5-6 months before commercial availability ends for defense buyers; NVIDIA has precedent for allocation programs during high-demand cycles.

Action: Lock long-term supply agreements with NVIDIA-authorized defense distributors by June 2026, specify Jetson Orin NX in all RFPs issued before allocation policy to grandfather contracts, and validate Qualcomm QRB5165 as drop-in replacement for next-generation designs.

🟡 Raspberry Pi 4B and RPi 5

Signal: 40,000+ Raspberry Pi 4B units confirmed diverted to Russian Geran-2/3 (Shahed-136/238) drones as 'Borscht Tracker V3' per GUR War & Sanctions portal; RPi 5 confirmed in Molniya-2R reconnaissance UAV; RPi-based tracker with mesh networking in Geran-5 jet drone.

Window: Diversion is ongoing and operational; U.S. export controls have not yet restricted Raspberry Pi, but congressional or BIS action is probable within 6-12 months as intelligence circulates.

Action: Remove Raspberry Pi 4B and RPi 5 from approved component lists for any defense or critical infrastructure programs immediately, audit existing inventory for serial number tracking and end-use compliance, substitute with U.S.-manufactured single-board computers (e.g., BeagleBone AI-64, Octavo OSD32MP1), and prepare for retroactive export compliance review if BIS issues emergency controls.

Predictions (3)

87% — H2 2026: FLIR Boson 640 tightens — 3 Boson units tracked, AeroV Switchblade ramp drives demand

Hedge: Execute firm fixed-price contract with 24-month Boson 640 delivery schedule by June 30, 2026; allocate $150K-$500K depending on program size; qualify Hadron 640R as alternate in parallel to avoid single-source lock.

82% — Q3 2026: Jetson Orin NX goes allocation-only for defense (13 boards in Forge DB at risk)

Hedge: Sign NVIDIA defense distributor agreement by June 2026 with minimum order quantity to secure allocation tier; budget 15% price premium for guaranteed supply; begin Qualcomm QRB5165 qualification testing as second source by May 2026.

79% — January 2027: FCC firmware cliff — 3 simultaneous deadlines ground uncertified gray zone drones

Hedge: Inventory all airframes lacking FCC firmware certification by May 2026; allocate 20% of fleet replacement budget to pre-positioning Blue UAS Framework alternatives; establish bridge contracts with certified vendors to activate in Q4 2026 if certification failures emerge.

Watch List (3)

⚠️ FCC firmware certification cliff in January 2027

79% probability that 3 simultaneous firmware deadlines ground uncertified gray zone drones; 4 regulatory/compliance flags matched with cross-correlation model confidence 0.89.

Trigger: FCC issues final firmware security requirements with compliance deadline; failure to certify by January 2027 would create hard grounding event for uncertified platforms including gray zone inventory still in field.

⚠️ Qualcomm QRB5165 successor entering Blue UAS Framework

80% probability in 2027; 5 of 25 companion computers (20% concentration) currently depend on QRB5165; successor platform would reset procurement timelines and create upgrade pressure.

Trigger: Qualcomm announces QRB5165 end-of-life or introduces next-generation defense-qualified chipset; Blue UAS Framework vendors begin design-ins, creating obsolescence risk for current-generation programs.

⚠️ Mesh radio supply bottleneck

81% probability in Q3-Q4 2026; 17 of 29 mesh radios (59% concentration) sourced from target vendors; only 29 NDAA-compliant options tracked in Forge DB.

Trigger: Single mesh radio vendor (likely Doodle Labs or Persistent Systems) announces allocation or lead time extension beyond 26 weeks; would cascade to UAS integrators with C2 delays.
